nodes.name: signtutor/DESIGN.md
observations: Design system document for SignTutor with 'Bioluminescent Studio' dark theme aesthetic
Defines OKLCH color tokens: bg-base #080c14, bg-surface #0f1420, bg-elevated #161d2e
Accent colors: primary teal (#00e5b0), secondary electric blue (#4f8cff), warm amber (#ffb347)
Typography: Syne (display), DM Sans (body), JetBrains Mono (labels/stats)
Spacing/radius tokens: sm 8px, md 14px, lg 20px, xl 28px
Motion guidelines: ease-out-quart/expo curves, no bounce/elastic, respects prefers-reduced-motion
Component patterns: glass cards with hover glow, pill badges with mono font, teal primary buttons

nodes.name: signtutor/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
observations: Architecture document covering stack, data flow, privacy model, CI/CD gates
Stack: Next.js 16 App Router + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4
ML runtime: onnxruntime-web (WASM) + MediaPipe Hands (CDN)
State: localStorage for prefs (signtutor.prefs) and progress (signtutor.progress)
Testing: Vitest (unit) + Playwright (E2E)
CI/CD: GitHub Actions with ML gates + frontend gates

Defines 8 architectural decisions: hybrid ML+rule classifier, auto-fallback, collision-aware verdict, first-frame anchoring, rotation-aug required, on-device lighting check, split latency HUD, mirrored webcam default
Data flow for static player: MediaPipe → normalize() → ONNX MLP + fingerStates/PATTERNS
Data flow for dynamic player: MediaPipe → 45-frame sliding buffer → normalizeSequence() → ONNX Transformer
Privacy model: no frames leave device, enforced by CSP connect-src
CI gates: JS↔Python parity, unit tests, typecheck, lint, E2E, Lighthouse a11y ≥95, ML training, robustness, NFR enforcement

nodes.name: signtutor/docs/ADR-002-dataset-choice.md
observations: ADR-002: Dataset choice, license stance, and signer demographics analysis
Status: Accepted (W1 — Real Data Foundation)
v1 used synthetic data; v2 must use real licensed data with documented provenance
Selected datasets in priority: ChicagoFSWild+ (26 classes, ~160 signers), WLASL2000 (2000 classes, 119 signers), MS-ASL (1000 classes, 222 signers, eval only), ASL Citizen (2731 signs, 52 signers)
Rejected: synthetic-only data, Surrey16/BSLdict (saved for multi-language beta)
Every dataset requires entry in ml/data/LICENSES.md; CI enforces grep gate

nodes.name: signtutor/docs/ADR-003-signer-disjoint-splits.md
observations: ADR-003: Signer-disjoint split strategy and CI audit
Status: Accepted (W1 — Real Data Foundation)
Split by signer identity, not by frame, to prevent signer leakage
Each dataset loader must emit signer_id per sample
CI runs ci/check_signer_disjoint.py on every PR; failure blocks merge
Rejected: random 70/20/10 split and stratified split by class (leak signers)
Consequence: lower headline accuracy but honest and reproducible metrics

nodes.name: signtutor/docs/ADR-004-calibration.md
observations: ADR-004: Calibration method — temperature scaling vs Platt vs isotonic regression
Status: Accepted (W6 — Confidence Calibration)
Decision: use temperature scaling on validation set after every training run
Fit scalar T on validation logits via LBFGS to minimize NLL
Bake T into ONNX graph as Div node before final softmax
Gate: ECE ≤ 0.05 on held-out test set; publish reliability diagram as CI artifact
Rejected: Platt scaling (calibrates only final boundary) and isotonic regression (overfits)
Adds ~1 parameter per model (T) with trivial runtime cost

nodes.name: signtutor/docs/ADR-005-personalization.md
observations: ADR-005: PersonalAdapter — encoder embedding format, adapter shape, and storage schema
Status: Accepted (W5 — On-Device Personalization)
Export Transformer with 128-d pooled encoder embedding output
PersonalAdapter: W (128 × n_classes), b (n_classes), vanilla SGD lr=0.01
Serialized to localStorage as { W, b, attempts }
Enabled after ≥ 20 confirmed attempts to avoid cold-start noise
Rejected: full fine-tuning (too slow), server-side (privacy), LoRA (over-engineered for 1KB budget)
Privacy gate must assert no fetch/XHR sends embeddings or adapter weights

nodes.name: signtutor/docs/ADR-006-nmm-fusion.md
observations: ADR-006: Holistic vs Hands+Face fusion and NMM feature strategy
Status: Accepted (W4 — Non-Manual Marker Scoring)
Decision: replace bare MediaPipe Hands with MediaPipe Holistic (single pass, ~12ms on WebGL2)
Face landmarks from Holistic used for NMM: BrowHead (72 floats → 3 logits), MouthHead (60 floats → 4 logits)
Feature flag NMM_SCORING gates NMM UI tile and extra ONNX inference
Latency budget: NMM on must not increase P50 by more than 50ms
Consent modal required before enabling NMM scoring
Rejected: separate sequential MediaPipe passes (2× cost) and custom face detector
holisticRunner.ts replaces handsRunner.ts as single landmark source

nodes.name: signtutor/docs/ADR-007-continuous-slr.md
observations: ADR-007: CTC vs Transformer-Decoder for continuous SLR
Status: Accepted (W8 — Continuous-Sign Research Preview)
Decision: CTC encoder-decoder Transformer for continuous SLR
Datasets: How2Sign or RWTH-PHOENIX-2014T; decode with greedy beam search (beam=5)
Gate: WER ≤ 0.50 on held-out test set
UI: /research/continuous behind interstitial explaining preview, no scoring, live transcription only
Rejected: Transformer-decoder with forced alignment (too slow) and pure CTC without beam search (bad WER)
Model excluded from production pipeline and sitemap; noindex,nofollow

nodes.name: signtutor/docs/ADR-008-language-bundles.md
observations: ADR-008: Per-language model bundle layout and lazy-loading strategy
Status: Accepted (W7 — Multi-Language Beta)
Each language gets ONNX model bundle under public/models/{lang}/
Curriculum, reference media, and labels loaded lazily on language switch
Prefetch triggers on hover/focus of language selector
Cache headers: Cache-Control public, max-age=31536000, immutable for versioned bundles
Rejected: monolithic bundle (too large) and CDN per language (network egress)
Build system must output versioned language bundles; loading state in selector

nodes.name: signtutor/docs/ADR-009-reference-media.md
observations: ADR-009: Sign reference media hosting — bundled vs CDN vs on-demand fetch
Status: Accepted (W1 — Sign Reference System)
In-house recordings bundled in public/refs/; CI downloads on cold cache
Lifeprint/ASL University GIFs bundled locally, attributed in REFERENCE_LICENSES.md
WLASL clips bundled locally (research-use only)
HandSpeak/SigningSavvy on-demand fetch with caching, blocked if no license entry
CI gate: grep for every media path in public/refs/, fail if no license entry
Rejected: hot-linking (fragile, license risk) and in-house only (cannot scale)

nodes.name: Bioluminescent Studio Design Theme
observations: Dark, focused, alive aesthetic described in DESIGN.md
Deep-sea research lab metaphor with glowing accents
Near-black backgrounds with blue undertone (#080c14 base)
Teal bioluminescent primary accent (#00e5b0)
Professional sign language coach at midnight setting

nodes.name: Hybrid ML + Rule Classifier
observations: Architectural decision #1: runs MLP and rule classifier concurrently at all times
MLP provides calibrated probabilities and discriminative top-K
Rule layer provides finger-level interpretability via fingerStates() + PATTERNS
Mode toggle switches which source is used for final verdict
Per-finger panel driven by rule layer, not MLP

nodes.name: On-Device Privacy Model
observations: No webcam frames leave the device — enforced by CSP connect-src restrictions
No hand-landmark data transmitted — normalize() runs entirely in browser
Only network requests on lesson pages are to load *.onnx model files
No analytics payloads or telemetry during practice
PersonalAdapter embeddings and weights never sent via fetch/XHR

nodes.name: Collision-Aware Verdict
observations: Architectural decision #3: handles visually identical letter pairs in synthetic training data
Same-collision-group neighbours (A=E=M=N=S=T) treated as 'close' not 'miss'
Friendly explanation shown to user for collision-group verdicts
COLLISION_GROUPS lives in src/lib/normalize.ts
Swapping to real data later makes this a no-op but logic stays

nodes.name: Normalization Pipeline
observations: Static normalization (normalize()): wrist-centred, scaled by wrist→middle-MCP distance, produces Float32Array(63)
Dynamic normalization (normalizeSequence()): centers entire sequence on wrist of frame 0, computes inter-frame deltas, produces Float32Array(45×126)
First-frame anchoring preserves global trajectory distinguishing signs like HELLO vs THANKS vs PLEASE
Per-frame independent normalization would destroy trajectory signal

nodes.name: CI/CD Gates
observations: Frontend gate: JS↔Python parity, unit tests, typecheck, lint, E2E, Lighthouse a11y ≥95
ML gate: training, robustness eval, NFR enforcement
Dataset grep gate: undocumented dataset paths fail build
Signer-disjoint CI: ci/check_signer_disjoint.py runs on every PR
Calibration gate: ECE ≤ 0.05 on held-out test set
Reference media gate: grep every media path against REFERENCE_LICENSES.md
